bullets.text: Israel has seized a castle in Lebanon, with Prime Minister Benjamin Netanyahu describing the capture as a "decisive shift" in its military campaign against Hezbollah.

The World Health Organization (WHO) has called for community cooperation and a ceasefire among armed groups to contain the Ebola outbreak in the Democratic Republic of Congo, where the death rate is estimated to be 30-50%.

Colombia is holding a presidential election that could redefine its relations with the US, following months of public recrimination between the current left-wing President Gustavo Petro and his US counterpart Donald Trump.

The United States Navy has shown interest in using the controversial Darwin ship lift, which is anticipated to be operational next year, according to Northern Territory Infrastructure Minister Bill Yan.
Capital city home prices in Australia have fallen, with experts predicting that the property slump could last a year and reduce values by 10%.
A person has died and another was seriously injured in a house fire in Werribee, Melbourne, which is currently under police investigation.
Wall Street investors and strategists are dismissing concerns about market overheating, betting on significant gains for AI-linked shares and a continued rally in US stocks.
Berkshire Hathaway Inc. is set to acquire Taylor Morrison Home Corp. in an all-cash deal valued at approximately $6.8 billion, as announced by both companies.
The notorious hukou system in China, which has long restricted rural migrants' lives, is being relaxed, potentially easing urban integration for this population.
